qa_pair.question: A developer is setting up Web3Auth with Wagmi in a React app. Using the MCP tools, fetch the React Quick Start example and examine the entry point (main.tsx). List the three wrapper provider components from outermost to innermost that are required for the Wagmi integration to work.

A developer built their app on Sapphire Devnet and is deploying to production using Sapphire Mainnet. Their beta testers report completely different wallet addresses. According to the SKILL's Key Derivation Rules, which THREE configuration parameters, if changed, will permanently produce a different wallet address? List them in the order they appear in the Key Derivation Rules section.
qa_pair.answer: Client ID, Sapphire network, connection configuration

qa_pair.question: After adding @web3auth/modal to a Vite project, a developer gets "global is not defined" errors in the browser console. Using the MCP tools, fetch the React Quick Start example and examine vite.config.ts. What value is the `global` key set to under the `define` option to fix this error?

A developer's app supports both Google login and Email Passwordless. Users who sign up with Google and later try Email Passwordless get a different wallet with no funds. Using the MCP tools, search the documentation for "grouped connections". What must be true about the user identifier fields across the sub-connections being grouped for the same user to get the same wallet address?
qa_pair.answer: The user identifier fields must be compatible (the same value) across sub-connections so the same user gets the same wallet address

qa_pair.question: Users on Safari report that clicking the social login button shows an infinite loader instead of the login popup. Using search_community, fetch the archived discussion at topic_id 1775. What UX mode does the community recommend using instead of popup mode to work around Safari's default popup blocking behavior?

A React Native developer using Expo asks whether they can use Expo Go for testing their Web3Auth integration. According to the Platform Quick Reference in the SKILL and the React Native SDK documentation, what is the correct answer, and what alternative does the documentation recommend instead of Expo Go?
qa_pair.answer: Expo Go is not supported; use a Custom Dev Client or EAS build

qa_pair.question: A developer set up custom JWT authentication with Firebase. Login works locally but intermittently fails in production despite a valid `exp` claim set to 1 hour. According to the SKILL and documentation, what specific JWT claim has a strict time constraint that could cause this, and within how many seconds of the current server time must it be?

A developer wants to use Web3Auth for server-side wallet operations in a Node.js backend (e.g. AI agents, automated transactions). Using the MCP tools and SKILL, what type of authentication does the Node.js SDK exclusively require, and what is the custody model implication since key reconstruction happens on the backend?
qa_pair.answer: Custom authentication only (JWT required); custodial to the dApp, non-custodial to Web3Auth

qa_pair.question: A React developer is using the standard `useConnect` and `useAccount` hooks from wagmi to manage Web3Auth login state, but `isConnected` is unreliable — sometimes showing disconnected even when the user is logged in. Using the MCP tools, fetch the React Quick Start example. What Web3Auth-specific hook does the example use instead for managing connection state?

A developer is building a Next.js App Router project. They add the Web3AuthProvider directly in their root layout.tsx but get a server component error because layout.tsx is a server component by default. According to the SKILL's Platform Quick Reference for React / Next.js, what must the Web3AuthProvider be placed inside to work with App Router?

A developer switches their Web3Auth config from Sapphire Devnet to Sapphire Mainnet for production, but continues testing on localhost. Social login silently fails with no clear error. According to the SKILL's Key Derivation Rules, why does login fail on localhost with Sapphire Mainnet?
qa_pair.answer: Sapphire Mainnet does not allow localhost; use Devnet for local development

qa_pair.question: A developer adds `useSFAKey: true` to their existing production Web3Auth configuration to enable wallet pregeneration. After deploying, all users report their wallets are empty and their funds are gone. According to the SKILL, why did this happen?
qa_pair.answer: Toggling useSFAKey in production permanently changes all existing user wallet addresses

qa_pair.question: A Flutter developer is integrating Web3Auth and needs to understand the SDK's initialization flow before calling login. Using get_sdk_reference for the Flutter platform with focus "main-class", find the Web3AuthFlutter class. What are the two static methods that must be called before login — one to set up the SDK configuration and one to restore an existing session? List them in the order they should be called.

A developer is building a Nuxt app with Web3Auth. The app crashes on the server because Web3Auth requires browser APIs that don't exist in SSR. According to the SKILL's Platform Quick Reference for Vue / Nuxt, what configuration must be added to the Nuxt plugin to prevent server-side rendering of Web3Auth?

A developer wants to change the branding, colors, and logo of the Web3Auth modal to match their product. They are about to write a `whiteLabel` config object in code. Using the MCP tools, fetch the whitelabel documentation page at https://docs.metamask.io/embedded-wallets/sdk/react/advanced/whitelabel/ and identify where basic modal appearance should first be configured before touching code.

A developer wants to enable Wallet Services (fiat on-ramp, swaps, WalletConnect interoperability) for their users. They start writing a `walletServicesConfig` object in code. Using the MCP tools, fetch the Wallet Services documentation at https://docs.metamask.io/embedded-wallets/sdk/react/advanced/wallet-services/ and identify the recommended primary way to configure Wallet Services without writing any code.
qa_pair.answer: Through the Embedded Wallets dashboard; manual SDK configuration is optional

qa_pair.question: A developer is upgrading their app from an older Web3Auth integration (previously known as Plug and Play / PnP or Core Kit). According to the SKILL, what are the old product names that are now deprecated, and what is the single unified name all SDKs now fall under?
qa_pair.answer: PnP, Core Kit, Torus are deprecated; everything is now MetaMask Embedded Wallets

qa_pair.question: A developer working on an Android app finds example repos described as "aggregate verifier" examples, but the Web dashboard and React documentation only use the term "grouped connections". Using the MCP tools, search for examples with auth_method "grouped" for Android and confirm: are "aggregate verifier" and "grouped connections" the same feature, just named differently across platforms?
qa_pair.answer: Yes, aggregate verifier and grouped connections are the same feature

qa_pair.question: A developer reading older Web3Auth documentation sees the term "verifier" used for setting up Google login or Firebase auth. The new dashboard now shows "connections" instead. According to the SKILL's Authentication Concepts section, what is the current name for what was previously called a "verifier", and what are the two identifiers it exposes that the old docs called "verifier ID" and "verifier name"?
qa_pair.answer: Connections (formerly verifiers); Auth Connection ID or name of the connection (verifier ID/ name)l and JWT user identifier field is user id
